---
title: "SONYC EDA"
author: "Lukas Lopez-Jensen"
date: "2026-03-31"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(ISOweek)
library(leaflet)
library(zoo)
library(forecast)
library(sf)
library(spdep)
library(councildown)
```


# Load and clean data
```{r}
sonyc <- read.csv("../Data/SONYC noise data annotations.csv")
sensors <- sonyc %>% distinct(sensor_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>% select(sensor_id, borough, block, latitude, longitude)
siren <- sonyc %>% select(sensor_id, audio_filename, annotator_id, borough, block, latitude, longitude, year, week, day, hour, X5.3_siren_presence, X5.3_siren_proximity)

siren_weekly <- siren %>%
  filter(X5.3_siren_presence == 1) %>%
  mutate(
    date = ISOweek2date(
      paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-", day + 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, week) %>%
  summarise(total_sirens = n(), .groups = "drop")

siren_monthly <- siren %>% filter(X5.3_siren_presence == 1) %>%
  mutate(date = ISOweek2date(paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-", day + 1))) %>% 
  mutate(month = lubridate::floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(total_sirens = n(), .groups = "drop")

siren_yearly <- siren %>% filter(X5.3_siren_presence == 1) %>%
  mutate(date = ISOweek2date(paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-", day + 1))) %>% 
  mutate(year = lubridate::floor_date(date, "year")) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(total_sirens = n(), .groups = "drop")

siren_hourly <- siren %>% filter(X5.3_siren_presence == 1) %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarise(total_sirens = n(), .groups = "drop")
```


# Map of sensors
```{r}
leaflet(data = sensors) %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>%
  setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) %>%
  addMarkers(
    ~longitude, ~latitude,
    clusterOptions = markerClusterOptions()
  ) %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius = 4,
    stroke = FALSE,
    fillOpacity = 0.7,
    popup = ~paste("Sensor ID:", sensor_id)
  )

# No sensors in The Bronx, 1 in Queens (close to a noise camera), 12 in Brooklyn (far from noise cameras).
# 43 of the 56 sensors are in Manhattan, and 32 of those are between Houston and 16th st. We get a half-decent match for 2 noise cameras.
# Sensor IDs 0 and 25 appear to have the same coordinates.
```


# Graph Siren Sounds (Weekly/Monthly/Yearly)
```{r}
ggplot(siren_weekly, aes(x = interaction(year, week), y = total_sirens, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Siren Counts in NYC",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Number of Sirens"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(siren_monthly, aes(x = month, y = total_sirens, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "4 months",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Siren Counts in NYC",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of Sirens"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(siren_yearly, aes(x = year, y = total_sirens, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Yearly Siren Counts in NYC",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Sirens"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(siren_hourly, aes(x = hour, y = total_sirens, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Siren Counts in NYC",
    x = "Time of Day (24-Hour Clock)",
    y = "Total Sirens Heard"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 170)) +
  theme_minimal()

# Identify week-level anomalies (rolling z-score)
siren_weekly <- siren_weekly %>%
  arrange(year, week) %>%
  mutate(
    roll_mean = rollmean(total_sirens, 8, fill = NA, align = "right"),
    roll_sd   = rollapply(total_sirens, 8, sd, fill = NA, align = "right"),
    z = (total_sirens - roll_mean) / roll_sd,
    spike = z > 2,
    date = ISOweek2date(paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-1"))
  )

ggplot(siren_weekly, aes(x = date, y = total_sirens)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_line(aes(y = roll_mean), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(data = subset(siren_weekly, spike), color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Siren Counts with Spikes Highlighted",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Number of Sirens"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
# No hotspots identified at the 4- or 6-week levels. 
# Using an 8-week average, 3 hotspots were identified, each in the fall:
# Weeks of 10/9/17, 11/5/18, and 9/30/19.
```

# Quick test for seasonality
```{r}
# Create a timeseries object
siren_ts <- ts(siren_weekly$total_sirens, frequency = 52, start = c(2016,11))

# Automatically assign best ARIMA model using AIC
model <- auto.arima(siren_ts, seasonal = TRUE)
summary(model)
# Best model doesn't incorporate seasonality -> No significant seasonality. One more check to be sure.

# Separate trend from seasonality and check for a pattern.
decomp <- stl(siren_ts, s.window = "periodic")
plot(decomp)
# Seasonal pattern is pretty noisy, no strong indication here either.

# Compare this to the models recommended by 311 dashboard for related complaints
fit_naive <- naive(siren_ts)
fit_tbats <- tbats(siren_ts)

# Naive model fails Ljung-Box: the immediate previous value cannot predict the next.
checkresiduals(fit_naive)
checkresiduals(model)
checkresiduals(fit_tbats)
```


# Map Siren Sounds
```{r}
sensor_rates <- siren %>%
  group_by(sensor_id, latitude, longitude) %>%
  summarise(
    total_obs = n(),
    total_sirens = sum(X5.3_siren_presence == 1),
    siren_rate = total_sirens / total_obs * 100,
    .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(rate_capped = pmin(siren_rate, 12), is_outlier = siren_rate > 12)

pal <- colorNumeric(pal_nycc("warm"), sensor_rates$rate_capped)

# Map siren prevalence: raw and as a % of sounds heard
leaflet(sensor_rates) %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>%
  setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    ~longitude, ~latitude,
    radius = ~sqrt(total_sirens) + 1,   # size = volume
    color = ~ifelse(is_outlier, "red", pal(rate_capped)),
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = ~ifelse(is_outlier, 2, 0),
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "Sensor ID: ", sensor_id, "<br>",
      "Total sirens: ", total_sirens, "<br>",
      "Siren Rate: ", paste0(round(siren_rate), "%")
    )
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    "bottomright",
    pal = pal,
    values = ~rate_capped,
    title = "Siren Rate"
  )

# ID 54 in Downtown Brooklyn is a clear outlier in terms of siren rate. Any other hotspots?
global_rate <- sum(sensor_rates$total_sirens) / sum(sensor_rates$total_obs)
sensor_rates <- sensor_rates %>%
  mutate(
    expected = total_obs * global_rate,
    variance = total_obs * global_rate * (1 - global_rate),
    z_score = (total_sirens - expected) / sqrt(variance),
    hotspot = z_score > 2.576   # ~99% threshold
  )
# 10 sensors identified
leaflet(sensor_rates) %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>%
  setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    ~longitude, ~latitude,
    radius = ~sqrt(total_sirens) + 1,   # size = volume
    color = ~ifelse(hotspot, "orange", pal(rate_capped)),
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = ~ifelse(hotspot, 2, 0),
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "Sensor ID: ", sensor_id, "<br>",
      "Siren Rate: ", paste0(round(siren_rate), "%"), "<br>",
      "Z-Score: ", round(z_score, 2),
      ifelse(hotspot, "<br><b>Hotspot</b>", "")
    )
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    "bottomright",
    pal = pal,
    values = ~rate_capped,
    title = "Siren Rate"
  )
# Lots of orange in lower Manhattan! Let's see if that cluster is significant.
```


# Test for clustering
```{r}
# Collapse sensors with duplicate locations for cluster analysis
sensor_collapsed <- sensor_rates %>%
  group_by(latitude, longitude) %>%
  summarise(
    total_sirens = sum(total_sirens),
    total_obs = sum(total_obs),
    siren_rate = total_sirens / total_obs * 100,
    n_sensors = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

sensor_sf <- st_as_sf(
  sensor_collapsed,
  coords = c("longitude", "latitude"),
  crs = 4326
)

# Project for distance-based neighbors (meters)
sensor_sf_proj <- st_transform(sensor_sf, 32618)  # UTM zone 18N

coords <- st_coordinates(sensor_sf_proj)

# Define neighbors within 500m
neighbors <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 500)

weights <- nb2listw(
  neighbors,
  style = "W",
  zero.policy = TRUE
)

# Getis-Ord Gi*
gi_star <- localG(
  sensor_sf_proj$siren_rate,
  listw = weights,
  zero.policy = TRUE
)

sensor_sf_proj$GiZ <- as.numeric(gi_star)

# Hotspot threshold:
# 99% significance level ~= z > 2.58
sensor_sf_proj$hotspot <- sensor_sf_proj$GiZ > 2.58

# Back to WGS84 for Leaflet
sensor_sf_leaflet <- st_transform(sensor_sf_proj, 4326)

# Color palette = siren rate
pal <- colorNumeric(
  palette = pal_nycc("warm"),
  domain = sensor_sf_leaflet$siren_rate
)

leaflet(sensor_sf_leaflet) %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>%
  setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    radius = ~sqrt(total_sirens) + 3,   # size = siren volume
    
    # Orange outline for hotspots, otherwise rate color
    color = ~ifelse(hotspot, "blue", pal(siren_rate)),
    
    # Keep fill tied to siren rate
    fillColor = ~pal(siren_rate),
    
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = ~ifelse(hotspot, 2, 0),
    
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<b>Siren Rate:</b> ",
      round(siren_rate), "%<br>",
      
      "<b>Total Sirens:</b> ",
      total_sirens, "<br>",
      
      "<b>Gi* Z-Score:</b> ",
      round(GiZ, 2),
      
      ifelse(
        hotspot,
        "<br><b style='color:blue;'>Hotspot (p < 0.01)</b>",
        ""
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    "bottomright",
    pal = pal,
    values = ~siren_rate,
    title = "Siren Rate (%)"
  )

# Indicates that Downtown Brooklyn might be a siren hotspot compared to Lower Manhattan!
```
